values.value:
# Configuring your billing portal
Each NetworkEntity__c that you want DH to invoice needs three things:
1. **EnableBillingDateTime__c populated.** This is the opt-in flag. Setting it to NOW says "DH may invoice this vendor". Clearing it (null) suspends invoicing without touching the relationship. 2. **BillingFrequencyPk__c set.** Allowed values: Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, On_Demand. The default is Monthly. Daily generates a Daily_Hours_Summary document instead of a full Invoice — useful for hourly contractors that need running tallies. 3. **BillingPortalUrl__c set (optional).** If you ship invoices to an external portal (Stripe, Bill.com, Lockstep, your own customer portal), put the per-vendor target URL here. DH writes the generated DeliveryDocument__c with this URL so downstream automation can route the invoice payload to the right place. Leave it blank if you only need DH-internal invoices.
Two additional knobs sit on DeliveryHubSettings__c:
- **InvoiceDueDateOffsetDays__c**: how many days past the invoice date the DueDateDate__c on the generated DeliveryDocument__c lands. Default 30. - **EnableStripePaymentsDateTime__c**: when populated, DH attaches a Stripe payment link to the invoice before it ships.
If you want to invoice a vendor manually instead of on a schedule, set BillingFrequencyPk to On_Demand and use the "Generate Invoice" quick action on the NetworkEntity record page.
